201	Note: the σ-component is the slice product `∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k))`, which is identified with
202	the wide fibre power `Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun k => f (σ k)))` via `widePullback_overX_eq_prod`
203	at the assembly site.  This is the project's chosen (slice-product) normal form for the components,
204	which minimizes the `HasWidePullback` instance bookkeeping in the induction. -/
205	noncomputable def widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
206	    {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Finite ι] {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S)
207	    [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
208	    [HasFiniteProducts (Over S)] [HasFiniteCoproducts (Over S)] :
209	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin 1 => Limits.Sigma.desc f))
210	      ≅ ∐ (fun σ : Fin 1 → ι => ∏ᶜ (fun k : Fin 1 => Over.mk (f (σ k)))) :=
211	  widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin 1 => Limits.Sigma.desc f) ≪≫
212	  productUniqueIso (fun _ : Fin 1 => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ≪≫
213	  (overSigmaDescIso f).symm ≪≫
214	  Sigma.whiskerEquiv (Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm
215	    (fun i => productUniqueIso
216	      (fun k : Fin 1 => Over.mk (f (((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm i) k))))
217	
218	
219	/-- One-sided distributivity in `Over S`'s underlying category, coproduct in the FIRST pullback
220	argument: `∐ᵢ pullback (gᵢ) b ≅ pullback (Sigma.desc g) b`.  Derived from the project's
221	`prod_coproduct_distrib` (coproduct-second form) by conjugating with `pullbackSymmetry`.
222	Project-local: consumed by `overProd_coproduct_distrib`'s structure-map compatibility. -/
223	noncomputable def coprodFirst_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C}
224	    (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
225	    (∐ fun i => pullback (g i) b) ≅ pullback (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b :=
226	  asIso (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackSymmetry (g i) b).hom)) ≪≫
227	    prod_coproduct_distrib B b g ≪≫ pullbackSymmetry b (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
228	
229	
230	/-- `prod_coproduct_distrib` is compatible with the first projection to the base of `a`:
231	its hom followed by `pullback.fst` is the descent of the per-summand `pullback.fst`. Project-local
232	compatibility lemma used to verify the structure-map condition of `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
233	lemma pcd_hom_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
234	    (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom ≫ pullback.fst a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
235	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst a (g i)) := by
236	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
237	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
238	  have hstep : (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom
239	      = (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
240	            pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])).inv)) ≫
241	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Sigma.ι Y i)) := by
242	    simp only [prod_coproduct_distrib, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_hom]
243	    congr 1
244	  rw [hstep]
245	  simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
246	  simp
247	  simp only [pullback.map]
248	  rw [pullback.lift_fst]
249	  simp
250	
251	/-- `prod_coproduct_distrib` compatibility with the second projection (the coproduct side):
252	its hom followed by `pullback.snd` descends to the per-summand `pullback.snd ≫ Sigma.ι`.
253	Project-local compatibility lemma for `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
254	lemma pcd_hom_snd {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
255	    (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom ≫ pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
256	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.snd a (g i) ≫ Sigma.ι Y i) := by
257	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
258	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
259	  have hstep : (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom
260	      = (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
261	            pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])).inv)) ≫
262	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Sigma.ι Y i)) := by
263	    simp only [prod_coproduct_distrib, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_hom]
264	    congr 1
265	  rw [hstep]
266	  simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
267	  rw [pullback.condition]
268	  simp
269	  simp only [pullback.map]
270	  rw [pullback.lift_snd_assoc]
271	  simp
272	
273	/-- `coprodFirst_distrib` compatibility with `pullback.fst` (the coproduct side): descends to the
274	per-summand `pullback.fst ≫ Sigma.ι`.  Project-local, used in `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
275	lemma cf_hom_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
276	    (coprodFirst_distrib B b g).hom ≫ pullback.fst (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b
277	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (g i) b ≫ Sigma.ι Y i) := by
278	  rw [coprodFirst_distrib]
279	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
280	  rw [pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst, pcd_hom_snd]
281	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun j => ?_)
282	  rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
283	    ← Category.assoc, pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
284	
285	private lemma overSigma_hom_eq {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] {S : C} {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
286	    (A : ι → Over S) :
287	    (∐ A).hom = (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom ≫
288	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
289	  haveI : HasColimit (Discrete.functor A ⋙ Over.forget S) :=
290	    hasColimit_of_iso (F := Discrete.functor (fun i => (A i).left))
291	      (Discrete.natIso (fun i => Iso.refl _))
292	  refine (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).inv_comp_eq.mp ?_
293	  rw [PreservesCoproduct.inv_hom]
294	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
295	  rw [ι_comp_sigmaComparison_assoc]
296	  show (Sigma.ι A i).left ≫ (∐ A).hom = _
297	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
298	  exact Over.w (Sigma.ι A i)
299	
300	/-- One-sided distributivity of the binary product over a finite coproduct in the slice category
301	`Over S` of a finitary pre-extensive category: `(∐ᵢ Aᵢ) ⨯ B ≅ ∐ᵢ (Aᵢ ⨯ B)`.  Built via
302	`Over.isoMk` from the `C`-level `prod_coproduct_distrib`, threading `Over.prodLeftIsoPullback`
303	and the coproduct-preservation of `Over.forget`.  This is the slice-product distributivity the
304	inductive step of `widePullback_coproduct_iso` consumes (blueprint `lem:overProd_coproduct_distrib`). -/
305	noncomputable def overProd_coproduct_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
306	    {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)] (A : ι → Over S) (B : Over S) :
307	    (∐ A) ⨯ B ≅ ∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B := by
308	  set pA := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A with hpA
309	  set pAB := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) (fun i => A i ⨯ B) with hpAB
310	  have hA : (∐ A).hom = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq A
311	  have hAB : (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
312	      = pAB.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq (fun i => A i ⨯ B)
313	  clear_value pA pAB
314	  have hAB' : pAB.inv ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
315	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := by
316	    rw [hAB]; simp
317	  have hcond : (∐ A).hom ≫ 𝟙 S = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
318	    rw [Category.comp_id]; exact hA
319	  refine Over.isoMk (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B ≪≫
320	    asIso (pullback.map (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom
321	      pA.hom (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S) hcond (by simp)) ≪≫
322	    (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).symm ≪≫
323	    asIso (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv)) ≪≫
324	    pAB.symm) ?_
325	  show _ ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom = ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
326	  have hR : ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
327	      = (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B).hom ≫ pullback.fst (∐ A).hom B.hom ≫ (∐ A).hom := by
328	    rw [← Over.w (prod.fst (X := ∐ A) (Y := B)), ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_fst, Category.assoc]
329	  have e3eq : (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).inv ≫
330	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom)
331	      = pullback.fst (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom ≫
332	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
333	    rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, cf_hom_fst]
334	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
335	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
336	  have e4eq : Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv) ≫
337	        Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom)
338	        = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom) := by
339	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
340	    rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
341	      Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, ← Over.w (prod.fst (X := A i) (Y := B)),
342	      ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_inv_fst_assoc]
343	  rw [hR]
344	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
345	  simp only [Iso.symm_hom]
346	  erw [hAB']
347	  rw [e4eq, e3eq]
348	  congr 1
349	  rw [← Category.assoc]
350	  simp only [pullback.map]
351	  rw [pullback.lift_fst, Category.assoc]
352	  exact congrArg _ hA.symm
353	
354	
355	/-- Right-handed one-sided distributivity in `Over S`: `A ⨯ (∐ᵢ Yᵢ) ≅ ∐ᵢ (A ⨯ Yᵢ)`. -/
356	noncomputable def overProd_coproduct_distrib_right {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
357	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
358	    {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)] (A : Over S) (Y : ι → Over S) :
359	    A ⨯ (∐ Y) ≅ ∐ fun i => A ⨯ Y i :=
360	  Limits.prod.braiding A (∐ Y) ≪≫ overProd_coproduct_distrib Y A ≪≫
361	    Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.prod.braiding (Y i) A)
362	
363	-- The inductive step chains six iso layers (`widePullback_overX_eq_prod`, `prodFinSuccIso`,
364	-- two distributivity isos, the reindex), whose combined `whnf` over the nested fibre powers
365	-- exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
366	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
367	/-- Coproduct distributes over the `(p+1)`-fold wide fibre power of the cover map `∐ᵢ Zᵢ ⟶ S`,
368	in slice-product normal form (blueprint `lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower`). -/
369	noncomputable def widePullback_coproduct_iso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
370	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {S : C} {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {Z : ι → C}
371	    (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
372	    [HasFiniteProducts (Over S)] [HasFiniteCoproducts (Over S)] :
373	    (p : ℕ) → (Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f))
374	      ≅ ∐ (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (f (σ k)))))
375	  | 0 => widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero f
376	  | (p+1) => by
377	      refine widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Limits.Sigma.desc f) ≪≫
378	        prodFinSuccIso (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ≪≫ ?_
379	      refine Limits.prod.mapIso (overSigmaDescIso f).symm
380	        ((widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f)).symm ≪≫
381	          widePullback_coproduct_iso f p) ≪≫ ?_
382	      refine overProd_coproduct_distrib (fun i => Over.mk (f i))
383	        (∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k))) ≪≫ ?_
384	      refine Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => overProd_coproduct_distrib_right (Over.mk (f i))
385	        (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k)))) ≪≫ ?_
386	      have e7 := Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
387	        (prodFinSuccIso (fun k : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons i τ k)))).symm))
388	      exact e7 ≪≫ coproduct_fibrePower_reindex p (fun σ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k)))
389	
390	end FinitaryPreExtensive
391	
392	/-- A coproduct indexed by `Option α` splits off its `none` summand:
393	`∐ Z ≅ Z none ⨿ (∐ a, Z (some a))`.  Project-local: the coproduct reassociation that drives the
394	`Option`-step of the finite-index induction in `pushPull_coprod_prod` (Mathlib has `sigmaSigmaIso`
395	for nested coproducts but no `Option`-split). -/
396	noncomputable def sigmaOptionIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type} (Z : Option α → C)
397	    [HasCoproduct Z] [HasCoproduct (fun a : α => Z (some a))]
398	    [HasBinaryCoproduct (Z none) (∐ fun a : α => Z (some a))] :
399	    (∐ Z) ≅ Z none ⨿ (∐ fun a : α => Z (some a)) where
400	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc
401	    (fun o => Option.rec Limits.coprod.inl
402	      (fun a => Limits.Sigma.ι (fun a : α => Z (some a)) a ≫ Limits.coprod.inr) o)
403	  inv := Limits.coprod.desc (Limits.Sigma.ι Z none)
404	    (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a => Limits.Sigma.ι Z (some a)))
405	  hom_inv_id := by
406	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
407	    rintro (_ | a)
408	    · simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inl_desc]
409	    · simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
410	  inv_hom_id := by
411	    apply Limits.coprod.hom_ext
412	    · simp [Limits.coprod.inl_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
413	    · rw [Category.comp_id]
414	      apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
415	      intro a
416	      simp [Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
417	
418	end CategoryTheory

420	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
421	
422	open Scheme.Modules
423	
424	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
425	
426	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Stub 1 geometric backbone
427	
428	The degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p])` is the `(p+1)`-fold
429	fibre power of the cover map `q = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f` over `X`.  Identifying it with the coproduct
430	`∐_σ (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` needs two geometric ingredients that Mathlib provides only in binary
431	form:
432	
433	* `widePullback_openImm_inter` — the wide pullback over `X` of a *finite* family of open
434	  immersions is the intersection of their open ranges (built here from `IsOpenImmersion.lift`
435	  and the wide-pullback universal property);
436	* the wide/iterated coproduct-distributes-over-fibre-power step (extensivity), still open.
437	-/
438	
439	open TopologicalSpace in
440	/-- Pointwise membership in a *finite* intersection of opens.  Project-local because Mathlib's
441	`Opens` infimum is interior-based, so the pointwise `mem_iInf` characterization only holds for
442	finite families. -/
443	private lemma mem_iInf_opens_of_finite {Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace Y] {κ : Type}
444	    [Finite κ] (f : κ → Opens Y) (x : Y) :
445	    x ∈ (⨅ i, f i : Opens Y) ↔ ∀ i, x ∈ f i := by
446	  rw [← SetLike.mem_coe,
447	    show ((⨅ i, f i : Opens Y) : Set Y) = ⋂ i, (f i : Set Y) from ?_]
448	  · simp
449	  · apply le_antisymm
450	    · exact Set.subset_iInter fun i => SetLike.coe_subset_coe.mpr (iInf_le f i)
451	    · have hopen : IsOpen (⋂ i, (f i : Set Y)) := isOpen_iInter_of_finite fun i => (f i).2
452	      have hW : (⟨⋂ i, (f i : Set Y), hopen⟩ : Opens Y) ≤ ⨅ i, f i :=
453	        le_iInf fun i => by intro y hy; exact Set.mem_iInter.mp hy i
454	      exact hW
455	
456	/-- The wide pullback over `X` of a finite family of open immersions `g k : Z k ⟶ X` is the
457	intersection open `⨅ k, (g k).opensRange`.  Project-local: Mathlib has the binary
458	`isPullback_opens_inf` but not this wide/iterated form, needed to identify each summand of the
459	distributed Čech-nerve backbone with a `coverInterOpen`.  (With `g k := 𝒰.f (σ k)` the target
460	open is exactly `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ`.) -/
461	noncomputable def widePullback_openImm_inter {κ : Type} [Finite κ]
462	    {Z : κ → Scheme.{u}} (g : (k : κ) → Z k ⟶ X) [∀ k, IsOpenImmersion (g k)] :
463	    widePullback X Z g ≅ (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).toScheme where
464	  hom := by
465	    refine IsOpenImmersion.lift (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι (WidePullback.base g) ?_
466	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
467	    rintro x ⟨w, rfl⟩
468	    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_iInf_opens_of_finite]
469	    intro k
470	    have hcomp : WidePullback.base g w = (g k) (WidePullback.π g k w) := by
471	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, WidePullback.π_arrow]
472	    rw [hcomp, ← SetLike.mem_coe, Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]
473	    exact Set.mem_range_self _
474	  inv := WidePullback.lift (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι
475	    (fun k => IsOpenImmersion.lift (g k) (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι (by
476	      rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι, ← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]
477	      exact SetLike.coe_subset_coe.mpr (iInf_le _ k)))
478	    (fun k => IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _)
479	  hom_inv_id := by
480	    apply WidePullback.hom_ext g
481	    · intro j
482	      rw [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_π,
483	        ← cancel_mono (g j), Category.assoc, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac,
484	        IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac, WidePullback.π_arrow]
485	    · rw [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_base, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac]
486	  inv_hom_id := by
487	    rw [← cancel_mono (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp,
488	      IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac, WidePullback.lift_base]
489	
490	/-- The cofan exhibiting `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` as the coproduct of the member arrows
491	`Over.mk (𝒰.f i)` in `Over X`.  Project-local: the coproduct cocone of the cover arrow. -/
492	noncomputable def coverArrowOverCofan (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
493	    Cofan (fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => Over.mk (𝒰.f i)) :=
494	  Cofan.mk (Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f))
495	    (fun i => Over.homMk (Sigma.ι 𝒰.X i) (by simp [Sigma.ι_desc]))
496	
497	/-- `coverArrowOverCofan` is a colimit: in `Over X` the cover arrow `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)`
498	is the coproduct of the member arrows.  Proved directly from the coproduct universal property in
499	`Scheme` (each leg is `Over.homMk (Sigma.ι …)`).  Project-local. -/
500	noncomputable def coverArrowOverIsColimit (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
501	    IsColimit (coverArrowOverCofan 𝒰) := by
502	  refine mkCofanColimit _
503	    (fun t => Over.homMk (Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left)) ?_)
504	    (fun t j => ?_) (fun t m hm => ?_)
505	  · change Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left) ≫ t.pt.hom = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f
506	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
507	    rw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Over.w]
508	    exact (Sigma.ι_desc 𝒰.f i).symm
509	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
510	    simp [coverArrowOverCofan, Sigma.ι_desc]
511	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
512	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
513	    have h := congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm i)
514	    simp only [coverArrowOverCofan, Cofan.inj, Cofan.mk,
515	      Discrete.natTrans_app, Over.homMk_left, Sigma.ι_desc] at h ⊢
516	    exact h
517	
518	/-- In `Over X`, the cover arrow `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` is the coproduct of the member
519	arrows `Over.mk (𝒰.f i)`.  Project-local component of the Stub-1 distributivity step: the inner
520	`∐ᵢ Uᵢ` of the fibre power, transported into `Over X`. -/
521	noncomputable def coverArrowOverSigmaIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
522	    (∐ fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => Over.mk (𝒰.f i)) ≅ Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) :=
523	  (coproductIsCoproduct _).coconePointUniqueUpToIso (coverArrowOverIsColimit 𝒰)

525	/-- Transport of the `m`-fold wide fibre power (constant family) of a single map `q : A ⟶ X` along
526	an isomorphism `w : B ≅ A` of the apex with `w.hom ≫ q = q'`: in `Over X` the backbones
527	`Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ => q))` and `Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ => q'))` agree.
528	Specialized to `Scheme` (the hom-universe must be pinned for the `rw`/`simp` on composites to fire).
529	Used in `cechBackbone_left_sigma` to transport the cover-arrow fibre power across the
530	universe-reduction reindexing `∐ 𝒰.X ≅ ∐ (𝒰.X ∘ e.symm)`. -/
531	noncomputable def widePullbackBaseCongr {A B : Scheme.{u}} (q : A ⟶ X) (q' : B ⟶ X)
532	    (w : B ≅ A) (hw : w.hom ≫ q = q') (m : ℕ) :
533	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q)) ≅
534	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q')) := by
535	  have hinv : w.inv ≫ q' = q := by rw [← hw, ← Category.assoc, w.inv_hom_id, Category.id_comp]
536	  refine Over.isoMk ?_ ?_
537	  · refine
538	      { hom := WidePullback.lift (WidePullback.base _)
539	          (fun k => WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin m => q) k ≫ w.inv)
540	          (fun k => by rw [Category.assoc, hinv]; exact WidePullback.π_arrow _ k)
541	        inv := WidePullback.lift (WidePullback.base _)
542	          (fun k => WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin m => q') k ≫ w.hom)
543	          (fun k => by rw [Category.assoc, hw]; exact WidePullback.π_arrow _ k)
544	        hom_inv_id := ?_
545	        inv_hom_id := ?_ }
546	    · apply WidePullback.hom_ext
547	      · intro k
548	        simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_π,
549	          WidePullback.lift_π_assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
550	      · simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_base]
551	    · apply WidePullback.hom_ext
552	      · intro k
553	        simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_π,
554	          WidePullback.lift_π_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
555	      · simp only [Over.mk_left, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_base]
556	  · simp [WidePullback.lift_base]

562	unfolding of `coverCechNerveOver = Over.lift …`, `coverCechNerve = augmentedCechNerve` and
563	`Arrow.cechNerve_obj`; all the identifications are definitional.  Project-local first step of

567	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)) :=

582	/- Planner strategy:
583	Goal: `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p]) ≅ ∐ fun σ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`
584	in `Over X`.
585	
586	Route:
587	(a) UNPACK `coverCechNerveOver`: it is `Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`,
588	    so the degree-`p` object is `Over.mk ((coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom.app (mk p))`.
589	    The underlying scheme is `(coverCechNerve 𝒰).left.obj (op (mk p))` — the `(p+1)`-fold
590	    fibre power of `coverArrow 𝒰 = Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` over `X`.
591	
592	(b) DISTRIBUTE: coproducts distribute over finite fibre products in `Scheme`
593	    (`Sigma.fiberProduct_sigma` or similar Mathlib anchor):
594	    `(∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ×_X ⋯ ×_X (∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ≅ ∐_σ (U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p})`
595	    for `σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`.
596	
597	(c) INTERSECT: each factor `U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p}` is the scheme-level intersection
598	    (fibre product of open immersions over `X`), which is the open subscheme
599	    `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` with structure map `Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
600	
601	(d) IDENTIFY: the structure map of the `σ`-component is the open immersion `j_σ`, and the

603	    equal to `∐_σ Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))` as an `Over X` object.
604	
605	Key Mathlib anchors:
606	- `Scheme.pullback_openCover_iSup` or sigma-fibre-product distribution in `Scheme`
607	- `Scheme.IsOpenImmersion.isPullback` (open immersions are pullback-stable)
608	- `ColimitCocone` machinery for the coproduct in `Over X`

610	Difficulty: MEDIUM — geometric bookkeeping, not sheaf theory. -/
611	noncomputable def cechBackbone_left_sigma (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
612	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≅
613	    ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
614	      Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
615	  -- Universe reduction: reindex the cover index `𝒰.I₀ : Type u` to `Fin n : Type 0`, since the
616	  -- extensivity primitive `isIso_sigmaDesc_fst` (in `widePullback_coproduct_iso`) is Type-0-only.
617	  set n := Nat.card 𝒰.I₀
618	  let e : 𝒰.I₀ ≃ Fin n := Finite.equivFin 𝒰.I₀
619	  let f' : (j : Fin n) → 𝒰.X (e.symm j) ⟶ X := fun j => 𝒰.f (e.symm j)
620	  -- reindexing iso on the cover coproduct, and its compatibility with the descent maps
621	  let wZ : (∐ fun j : Fin n => 𝒰.X (e.symm j)) ≅ ∐ 𝒰.X :=
622	    Sigma.whiskerEquiv e.symm (fun j => Iso.refl _)
623	  have hwZ : wZ.hom ≫ Sigma.desc 𝒰.f = Sigma.desc f' := by
624	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun j => ?_)
625	    simp only [wZ, Sigma.whiskerEquiv, Iso.refl_inv, Sigma.ι_comp_map'_assoc, Category.id_comp,
626	      Sigma.ι_desc, f']
627	  -- reindex the σ-coproduct back from `Fin (p+1) → Fin n` to `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`
628	  let reIdx : (∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → Fin n => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f' (σ k))) ≅
629	      ∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (𝒰.f (τ k)) :=
630	    Sigma.whiskerEquiv (f := fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → Fin n => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f' (σ k)))
631	      (g := fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (𝒰.f (τ k)))
632	      (Equiv.arrowCongr (Equiv.refl (Fin (p + 1))) e.symm) (fun σ => Iso.refl _)

634	    widePullbackBaseCongr (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) (Sigma.desc f') wZ hwZ (p + 1) ≪≫
635	    FinitaryPreExtensive.widePullback_coproduct_iso f' p ≪≫ reIdx ≪≫
636	    Sigma.mapIso (fun σ => coverInterProdIso 𝒰 σ)

640	/-- A morphism in `X.Modules` is an isomorphism as soon as its image under the forgetful functor
641	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` to presheaves of abelian groups is one.  `toPresheaf` reflects
642	isomorphisms (it factors through fully faithful functors), so this is immediate.  Project-local L1
643	reflection wrapper for the Stub-2 disjoint-union chain (blueprint `lem:isIso_modules_of_toPresheaf`). -/
644	theorem isIso_modules_of_toPresheaf {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
645	    [IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)] : IsIso φ :=
646	  isIso_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)

648	/-- If `BinaryFan.mk α β` is a limit, then the canonical comparison map `prod.lift α β` into the
649	chosen binary product is an isomorphism.  Project-local categorical helper for the disjoint-cover
650	decomposition. -/
651	private lemma isIso_prodLift_of_isLimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {P Q T : C}
652	    [HasBinaryProduct P Q] {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q}
653	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk α β)) : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift α β) := by
654	  have heq : (h.conePointUniqueUpToIso (prodIsProd P Q)).hom = Limits.prod.lift α β := by
655	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
656	    · have := h.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp (prodIsProd P Q) ⟨WalkingPair.left⟩
657	      simpa [Limits.prod.lift_fst] using this
658	    · have := h.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp (prodIsProd P Q) ⟨WalkingPair.right⟩
659	      simpa [Limits.prod.lift_snd] using this
660	  rw [← heq]; infer_instance

662	/-- If a functor `G` preserves the binary product `P ⨯ Q` and the mapped binary fan
663	`BinaryFan.mk (G.map α) (G.map β)` is a limit, then `G.map (prod.lift α β)` is an isomorphism.
664	Project-local: combines `prodComparison` naturality with `isIso_prodLift_of_isLimit`. -/
665	private lemma isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D]
666	    {P Q T : C} [HasBinaryProduct P Q] (G : C ⥤ D) [PreservesLimit (pair P Q) G]
667	    {α : T ⟶ P} {β : T ⟶ Q} [HasBinaryProduct (G.obj P) (G.obj Q)]
668	    (h : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map α) (G.map β))) :
669	    IsIso (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β)) := by
670	  have hcomp : G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β) ≫ prodComparison G P Q
671	      = Limits.prod.lift (G.map α) (G.map β) := by
672	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
673	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_fst, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_fst,
674	        Limits.prod.lift_fst]
675	    · rw [Category.assoc, prodComparison_snd, ← G.map_comp, Limits.prod.lift_snd,
676	        Limits.prod.lift_snd]
677	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (G.map α) (G.map β)) := isIso_prodLift_of_isLimit h
678	  haveI : IsIso (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β) ≫ prodComparison G P Q) := hcomp ▸ this
679	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right (G.map (Limits.prod.lift α β)) (prodComparison G P Q)

683	variable {A B : Scheme.{u}}

685	/-- The binary disjoint-cover decomposition comparison map of a module sheaf on a coproduct
686	scheme: the pair of restriction-to-component units. -/
687	private noncomputable def coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
688	    M ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
689	          (M.restrict (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)) ⨯
690	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
691	          (M.restrict (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)) :=
692	  prod.lift ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).unit.app M)
693	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)).unit.app M)

695	/- **Status (iter-062): `isIso_coprodDecompMap` is DONE axiom-clean (below).**
696	
697	The disjoint-cover decomposition iso `IsIso (coprodDecompMap M)` — the residual leaf the iter-061
698	handoff identified — is now proved (see `isIso_coprodDecompMap`): `isProductOfDisjoint` on the Ab
699	sheaf `M.presheaf` (opens `inl ''ᵁ inl⁻¹V`, `inr ''ᵁ inr⁻¹V`), reflected to `ModuleCat` through
700	`SheafOfModules.evaluation V` + `forget₂ (ModuleCat _) Ab`, then `isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit`.
701	
702	**Remaining for L2 `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` (NOT just the leaf — the `q_*`-assembly).**
703	The L2 statement is in `X.Modules` about `pushPullObj F (Over.mk q)` (`q = coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom`),
704	NOT directly about `coprodDecompMap M`.  Reduction (every step verified to exist; the per-leg
705	coherence (★) is the work):
706	  • Let `M := q^* F`.  The chain iso
707	      `chainIso := (pushforward q).mapIso (asIso (coprodDecompMap M))`         -- q_*M ≅ q_*(P⨯Q)
708	        `≪≫ PreservesLimitPair.iso (pushforward q) P Q`                        -- ≅ q_*P ⨯ q_*Q
709	        `≪≫ Limits.prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁`                                  -- ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ Y₁
710	    where `idiso₀ : (pushforward q).obj P ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀` is
711	      `(pushforward q).mapIso ((pushforward inl).mapIso innerIso₀) ≪≫ eqToIso hcod₀`,
712	      `innerIso₀ := (restrictFunctorIsoPullback inl).app M ≪≫ (pullbackComp inl q).app F
713	                      ≪≫ (pullbackCongr w₀).app F`,  `hcod₀` via `congrArg (·_* (·^*F)) w₀`.
714	    KEY DEFEQ (verified iter-062 by `rfl`): `q_*(inl_* N) = (pushforward (inl ≫ q)).obj N` — i.e.
715	    `pushforwardComp` is identity-on-objects (cf. `pushforwardComp_hom_app_app = 𝟙`), so `hcod₀`'s
716	    `pushforwardComp` leg is rfl and only the `w₀`-transport remains.
717	  • To make the iso CANONICAL (downstream Stub 4/5 need it), define
718	      `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod := asIso (prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr))`,
719	    proving `IsIso` by `prod.hom_ext`-matching that map against `chainIso.hom` (manifestly iso).
720	  • (★) Per-leg coherence to discharge (leg 0; leg 1 symmetric):
721	      `pushPullMap F overInl = (pushforward q).map u₀ ≫ idiso₀.hom`,  `u₀ = restrictAdjunction.unit`.
722	    Tools (all verified present): `pushPullMap_eq_raw` + `rawPushPullMap_self_gen` rewrite the LHS to
723	    `(pushforward q).map (η^{inl}_M ≫ inl_*((pullbackComp inl q).hom.app F)) ≫ eqToHom₀`;
724	    `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` (note `restrictFunctorIsoPullback = leftAdjointUniq`)
725	    rewrites `η^{inl}_M = u₀ ≫ inl_*((restrictFunctorIsoPullback inl).hom.app M)`; the rest collapses
726	    to an `eqToHom = eqToHom` (proof-irrelevant) after `Functor.map_comp` + `eqToHom_map`.
727	  Then `pushPull_coprod_prod` (finite induction) and `pushPull_sigma_iso` (Stub 2, line ~803) chain
728	  the binary iso; closing Stub 2 needs only the iso (canonical characterization matters at Stub 4/5).
729	
730	The comparison map `coprodDecompMap` and `isIso_coprodDecompMap` are axiom-clean below. -/
731	
732	/-- The binary disjoint-cover decomposition comparison map `coprodDecompMap M` is an isomorphism:
733	a module sheaf on `A ⨿ B` is the product of its restrictions-to-component pushforwards.  This is the
734	binary disjoint-union sheaf decomposition (`TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` on the underlying
735	abelian sheaf), reflected back to `(A ⨿ B).Modules`.  Project-local leaf of the Stub-2 chain. -/
736	private theorem isIso_coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
737	    IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := by
738	  rw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
739	  intro U
740	  set ι₀ : A ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inl with hι₀
741	  set ι₁ : B ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inr with hι₁
742	  set W₀ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₀ ''ᵁ ι₀ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₀
743	  set W₁ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₁ ''ᵁ ι₁ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₁
744	  have h₂ : W₀ ⊓ W₁ = ⊥ := by
745	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
746	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
747	    rw [← inf_inf_distrib_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).inf_eq_bot, bot_inf_eq]
748	  have h₁ : W₀ ⊔ W₁ = U := by
749	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
750	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
751	    rw [← inf_sup_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).sup_eq_top, top_inf_eq]
752	  -- the two restriction-unit legs at `U`, identified as presheaf restriction maps
753	  set u₀ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₀).unit.app M with hu₀
754	  set u₁ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₁).unit.app M with hu₁
755	  have hleg₀ : u₀.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₀.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
756	    rw [hu₀, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
757	  have hleg₁ : u₁.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
758	    rw [hu₁, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
759	  -- the disjoint-union limit on the underlying abelian sheaf of `M`, transported to `Γ(M, U)`
760	  have LimAb : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (u₀.app U) (u₁.app U)) := by
761	    have L := TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint
762	      (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab _) W₀ W₁ h₂
763	    refine L.ofIsoLimit (Cone.ext (M.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (congrArg Opposite.op h₁))) ?_)
764	    rintro ⟨(_ | _)⟩
765	    · show M.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_sup_left).op
766	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₀.app U
767	      rw [hleg₀, show (homOfLE (le_sup_left : W₀ ≤ W₀ ⊔ W₁)).op
768	            = eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁) ≫ (homOfLE (ι₀.image_preimage_le U)).op
769	          from Subsingleton.elim _ _, M.presheaf.map_comp]
770	      rfl
771	    · show M.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_sup_right).op
772	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₁.app U
773	      rw [hleg₁, show (homOfLE (le_sup_right : W₁ ≤ W₀ ⊔ W₁)).op
774	            = eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁) ≫ (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op
775	          from Subsingleton.elim _ _, M.presheaf.map_comp]
776	      rfl
777	  -- reflect the limit up to `ModuleCat` through the evaluation functor
778	  set G := SheafOfModules.evaluation (R := (A ⨿ B).ringCatSheaf) (Opposite.op U) with hGdef
779	  have LimMod : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map u₀) (G.map u₁)) := by
780	    refine isLimitOfReflectsOfMapIsLimit (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat)
781	      (G.map u₀) (G.map u₁) ?_
782	    exact LimAb
783	  -- conclude isomorphy of the comparison map at `U`
784	  haveI key : IsIso (G.map (coprodDecompMap M)) :=
785	    isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit G LimMod
786	  have happ : (coprodDecompMap M).app U
787	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (G.map (coprodDecompMap M)) := rfl
788	  rw [happ]
789	  exact Functor.map_isIso _ _

791	/-! ### L2 `q_*`-coherence chain: push–pull turns a coproduct into a product
792	
793	The binary disjoint-cover decomposition `isIso_coprodDecompMap` is upgraded to a statement about
794	the push–pull object `pushPullObj F (Over.mk q)` (`q = coprod.desc pA pB`).  The single substantive
795	node is the per-leg coherence `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★): the contravariant push–pull map of
796	the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q` is, up to the canonical leg iso
797	`pushPullCoprodLegIso`, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit.  This lets the
798	canonical comparison `prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)` be matched against
799	the manifestly-iso chain through `coprodDecompMap`. -/

801	/-- The canonical leg iso identifying `q_*(c_*(M.restrict c))` (a factor of `q_*(P ⨯ Q)`, where

823	private lemma pushPull_binary_leg_coherence {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
824	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
825	    pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q) =
826	      (pushforward q).map
827	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) ≫
828	        (pushPullCoprodLegIso q c pC wC F).hom := by
829	  have hraw : pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q)
830	      = rawPushPullMap c q pC wC F := rfl
831	  rw [hraw, rawPushPullMap_self_gen]
832	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≫
833	        (pushforward c).map
834	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).hom.app
835	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) =
836	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction c).unit.app
837	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) :=
838	    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ _
839	  subst wC
840	  simp only [pushPullCoprodLegIso, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, eqToIso.hom,
841	    Iso.app_hom, Category.comp_id,
842	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr, eqToIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, NatTrans.id_app]
843	  rw [← hLAU]
844	  simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl

854	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_binary_coprod_prod`). -/
855	noncomputable def pushPull_binary_coprod_prod (F : X.Modules) (Y₀ Y₁ : Over X) :
856	    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom)) ≅
857	      pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ := by
858	  set q : Y₀.left ⨿ Y₁.left ⟶ X := Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom with hq
859	  set M := (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F with hM
860	  have wInl : (Limits.coprod.inl : Y₀.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₀.hom := Limits.coprod.inl_desc _ _
861	  have wInr : (Limits.coprod.inr : Y₁.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₁.hom := Limits.coprod.inr_desc _ _
862	  set overInl : Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl wInl with hoverInl
863	  set overInr : Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr wInr with hoverInr
864	  haveI : IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := isIso_coprodDecompMap M
865	  -- The per-leg identifications.  Their codomains are pinned to `pushPullObj F Y₀`/`Y₁`
866	  -- (defeq to the `pushPullObj F (Over.mk Y₀.hom)` produced by `pushPullCoprodLegIso`); the
867	  -- syntactic pin is essential so the `Category.assoc`/`prod.map_fst` rewrites below can match
868	  -- the trailing `prod.fst` on `pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁`.
869	  set idiso₀ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inl).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inl))
870	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ :=
871	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F with hidiso0
872	  set idiso₁ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inr).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inr))
873	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
874	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F with hidiso1
875	  have hcoh0 : pushPullMap F overInl
876	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inl).unit.app M)
877	          ≫ idiso₀.hom := by
878	    rw [hidiso0]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F
879	  have hcoh1 : pushPullMap F overInr
880	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inr).unit.app M)
881	          ≫ idiso₁.hom := by
882	    rw [hidiso1]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F
883	  set chainIso : (pushforward q).obj M ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
884	    (pushforward q).mapIso (asIso (coprodDecompMap M)) ≪≫
885	      Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso (pushforward q) _ _ ≪≫
886	      Limits.prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁ with hchain
887	  -- Match the canonical comparison against the reference chain entirely through `prod.lift`
888	  -- identities (`prod.lift_map`, `prod.comp_lift`, and `prodComparison = prod.lift (q_* fst)
889	  -- (q_* snd)`), avoiding any `≫ prod.fst` projection that the surrounding pushforward objects
890	  -- make awkward to reassociate.
891	  have hcmp : Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr) = chainIso.hom := by
892	    rw [hcoh0, hcoh1, hchain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
893	      Limits.prod.mapIso_hom, Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso_hom]
894	    show Limits.prod.lift _ _ =
895	      (pushforward q).map (coprodDecompMap M) ≫
896	        Limits.prod.lift ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.fst) ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.snd)

898	    rw [Limits.prod.lift_map, Limits.prod.comp_lift, ← Functor.map_comp_assoc,
899	      ← Functor.map_comp_assoc, coprodDecompMap, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.prod.lift_snd]
900	    rfl
901	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)) := by

36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97

100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106

115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142

155	- ...
156

160

162	- ...

167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.

If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. 12	
13	This file establishes `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`:
14	
15	1. `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`: for an open immersion `j : U ↪ X` of an
16	   affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`, the higher direct images `R^q j_* H` vanish
17	   for `q ≥ 1` and any quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H`.
18

20	   morphism `f : X ⟶ S` and quasi-coherent `H` on `U`, there is a canonical isomorphism
21	   `R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H`.

31	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
32	
33	namespace CategoryTheory.Functor
34	
35	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
36	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then `X`
37	is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` + `Functor.map_injective`.)
38	
39	Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory).  A copy of the same lemma in
40	`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`; reproduced here because that sibling file is not in this
41	file's import chain. -/
42	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
43	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms D]
44	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
45	    (h : Limits.IsZero (F.obj Y)) : Limits.IsZero Y := by
46	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
47	  apply F.map_injective
48	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
49	  exact h
50	
51	end CategoryTheory.Functor
52	
53	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a sectionwise-locally-zero presheaf -/
54	
55	namespace CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology
56	
57	open Opposite in
58	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf whose sections are locally zero vanishes.**
59	If for every object `U` and every section `s : Q(U)` there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U`
60	on which `s` restricts to `0`, then the sheafification of `Q` is a zero object.
61	
62	This is the *sectionwise* (local-injectivity) strengthening of
63	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero`: that lemma requires the *objectwise* vanishing
64	`Q.obj (op V) = 0` for every member `V` of a single covering sieve, which forces the
65	vanishing to hold on a downward-closed family.  Here only the restricted *section* need
66	vanish, so the witnessing sieve can be generated by a cofinal *basis* of "good" opens
67	(e.g. the affine opens, where the section group vanishes) even though the basis itself is
68	not downward closed.  The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero
69	presheaf) is read off from the hypothesis applied to `x - y`; local surjectivity is free.
70	Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
71	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
72	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
73	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
74	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
75	    (h : ∀ (U : C) (s : ToType (Q.obj (op U))), ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
76	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) s = 0) :
77	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
78	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
79	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
80	    intro x
81	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
82	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
83	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
84	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
85	    refine ⟨?_⟩
86	    intro X x y _
87	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop (x - y)
88	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
89	    intro V g hg
90	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
91	    have h0 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) (x - y) = 0 := hSzero hg
92	    rw [map_sub] at h0
93	    exact sub_eq_zero.mp h0
94	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
95	    refine ⟨?_⟩
96	    intro U s
97	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
98	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
99	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
100	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
101	  exact isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ
102	
103	end CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology

109	-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` so that the
110	-- `Ext`-based transport below resolves it without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search.
111	attribute [local instance] hasExtModules
112	
113	variable {U X S : Scheme.{u}}
114	
115	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — corepresentability of the sections functor
116	
117	The sections functor `Γ(V, -)` on `X.Modules` is corepresented by the Form-B object
118	`jShriekOU V`.  Consequently its right derived functors agree, degreewise, with those of the
119	Hom-functor `Hom(jShriekOU V, -)` — the homological half of the identification
120	`Rᵠ Γ(V, -) ≅ Ext^q(jShriekOU V, -)` (the deep "Bridge (1)/(2)" connecting the higher-direct-image
121	presheaf homology to absolute cohomology). -/
122	
123	/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf** (reproduced here; the version
124	in `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` is `private`). Composing a map `jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁` with `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂`
125	corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on sections over `V`. -/
126	private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
127	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
128	    jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
129	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (op V)))
130	          (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁ h) := by
131	  have hs : sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
132	        = sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
133	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
134	        (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
135	  change freeYonedaHomEquiv V _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
136	  rw [hs, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
137	  rfl
138	
139	/-- `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` is additive (it is the additive composite
140	`forget ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙)`). Project-local instance so `sectionsFunctor` is additive. -/
141	noncomputable instance toPresheafOfModules_additive :
142	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).Additive :=
143	  inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
144	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive
145	
146	/-- The sections functor `Γ(V,-) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab` is additive. Project-local instance (the flat
147	triple composite does not infer additivity automatically). -/
148	noncomputable instance sectionsFunctor_additive (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
149	    (AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).Additive := by
150	  unfold AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor
151	  haveI i2 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
152	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (op V)).Additive :=
153	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
154	  exact Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
155	
156	/-- **The sections functor `Γ(V,-)` is corepresented by `jShriekOU V`.** A natural isomorphism
157	`sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V))`, assembled from the additive
158	corepresentability bijection `jShriekOU_homEquiv` and its naturality. Project-local: the functorial
159	upgrade of `jShriekOU_homEquiv`, the geometric input to the absolute-cohomology bridge. -/
160	noncomputable def sectionsFunctorCorepIso (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
161	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V)) :=
162	  NatIso.ofComponents
163	    (fun F => (AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F)).symm)
164	    (by
165	      intro F₁ F₂ g
166	      ext s
167	      simp only [AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso, comp_apply, Iso.symm_hom, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
168	        AddEquiv.coe_toAddMonoidHom]
169	      rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_eq]
170	      change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).map g)) s
171	        = jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s ≫ g)
172	      rw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s) g, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
173	      rfl)
174	
175	/-- **Right derived functors transport along a natural isomorphism of additive functors.**
176	Project-local: assembled from `NatTrans.rightDerived` of `τ.hom`/`τ.inv` and functoriality
177	(`NatTrans.rightDerived_comp`, `NatTrans.rightDerived_id`). -/
178	noncomputable def rightDerivedNatIso {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Abelian C]
179	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [Abelian D] {F G : C ⥤ D} [F.Additive] [G.Additive]
180	    (τ : F ≅ G) (n : ℕ) : F.rightDerived n ≅ G.rightDerived n where
181	  hom := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.hom n
182	  inv := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.inv n
183	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.hom_inv_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
184	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.inv_hom_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
185	
186	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext` transport along the spectrum equivalence (Need #1)
187	
188	The whole-scheme spectrum isomorphism `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`, a genuine iso because
189	`U` is affine) induces an *equivalence* of module categories `U.Modules ≌ (Spec Γ U).Modules`,
190	assembled from `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` along the iso and its coherences. An equivalence functor
191	is fully faithful, exact, and preserves injective objects, so
192	`Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` upgrades `Ext.mapExactFunctor` along it
193	to an *isomorphism* of `Ext`-groups. This transports the residual general-affine-open Serre
194	vanishing for `U` to the `Spec`-developed `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`.
195	
196	The pushforward-of-an-iso construction is generic in the scheme isomorphism `φ : X ≅ Y`. -/
197	
198	/-- **A scheme isomorphism induces an equivalence of module categories.** For `φ : X ≅ Y`, the
199	pushforward `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` is an equivalence with quasi-inverse the pushforward
200	along `φ.inv`; the unit/counit are supplied by the
201	`pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` coherences. Project-local: Mathlib packages the
202	pushforward functor and its coherences but not the equivalence assembled from an iso (the genuine
203	equivalence used to transport `Ext` for Need #1). -/
204	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
205	    X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules :=
206	  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk
207	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.inv)
208	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.hom_inv_id.symm ≪≫
209	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.hom φ.inv).symm)
210	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.inv φ.hom ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.inv_hom_id ≪≫
211	      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)
212	
213	/-- The functor of `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` is (defeq) the additive `pushforward φ.hom`. -/
214	noncomputable instance pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
215	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.Additive :=
216	  inferInstanceAs (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom).Additive
217	
218	/-- **`Ext` transport along a scheme isomorphism.** For `φ : X ≅ Y` and `A B : X.Modules`, the
219	equivalence `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` (whose functor is fully faithful, exact, and injective-object
220	preserving) upgrades the additive map `Ext.mapExactFunctor` to an isomorphism of `Ext`-groups
221	`Ext A B n ≃+ Ext (φ_* A) (φ_* B) n`, via `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`.
222	Project-local: the `Ext`-isomorphism half of Need #1 (the spectrum-equivalence transport). -/
223	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
224	    [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (A B : X.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
225	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
226	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj A)
227	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj B) n :=
228	  AddEquiv.ofBijective ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.mapExtAddHom A B n)
229	    (Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects
230	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor A B n)
231	
232	/-- **Need #1 — `Ext` transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence.** For an affine scheme
233	`U`, the canonical iso `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`) induces, for every `q`, an isomorphism
234	`Ext^q_U(A, B) ≅ Ext^q_{Spec Γ U}(φ_* A, φ_* B)`. In particular the residual `Ext` over `U` vanishes
235	iff its transport over `Spec Γ U` does. Project-local target of `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`.
236	
237	The remaining identification of the transported arguments — `φ_*(jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V'` for
238	`V'` the (affine) image open, and quasi-coherence of `φ_* H` — is handed off (see the task-result
239	file); it is orthogonal to this transport and is the geometric naturality of `j_!` under a scheme
240	iso. -/
241	noncomputable def modulesIsoSpecExtTransport (U : Scheme.{u}) [IsAffine U]
242	    [EnoughInjectives U.Modules] (A B : U.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
243	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
244	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext
245	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj A)
246	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj B) n :=
247	  Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv U.isoSpec A B n

249	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext`-vanishing ⟹ coyoneda right-derived vanishing
250	
251	The homological-algebra half of the relative-affine vanishing argument (Bridge (1)/(2) remainder).
252	The `q`-th right derived functor of the additive Hom-functor `Hom(P,-) = preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`,
253	evaluated at `H`, is computed (via the chosen injective resolution `I•` of `H`,
254	`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) as the homology at degree `q` of the cochain complex of
255	abelian groups `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)`.  By `InjectiveResolution.extMk` / `extMk_eq_zero_iff`, that homology
256	*is* `Ext P H q`: a degree-`q` cocycle is a map `f : P ⟶ Iq` with `f ≫ d = 0`, and it is a coboundary
257	iff the class `extMk f` vanishes in `Ext`.  Hence if every class of `Ext P H q` vanishes (`q ≥ 1`),
258	the Hom-complex is exact at `q` and the right-derived object is a zero object.  Project-local: Mathlib's
259	`Ext` is built on the derived category, not on `Functor.rightDerived`, so this bridge is absent. -/
260	
261	/-- The differential of the Hom-cochain-complex `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)` (the image of a cochain complex `K`
262	under `preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`) acts by post-composition with the differential of `K`. -/
263	private lemma preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply {C : Type*} [Category C]
264	    [Preadditive C] (P : C) (K : CochainComplex C ℕ) (i j : ℕ) (g : P ⟶ K.X i) :
265	    (((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).d i j g
266	      = g ≫ K.d i j := by
267	  rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d]
268	  rfl
269	
270	/-- **`Ext`-vanishing forces the coyoneda right-derived object to vanish.** If every class of
271	`Ext P H q` is zero (`q ≥ 1`), then `(Hom(P,-)).rightDerived q` is a zero object at `H`. The
272	homological-algebra bridge between the project's `Ext`-based Serre vanishing and the
273	`Functor.rightDerived` description of the higher-direct-image presheaf homology. Project-local. -/
274	lemma isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
275	    [Abelian C] [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C] (P H : C) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q)
276	    (hExt : ∀ e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext P H q, e = 0) :
277	    IsZero (((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)).rightDerived q).obj H) := by
278	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hq.ne'
279	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
280	    (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)) (p + 1))
281	  set R := injectiveResolution H with hR
282	  refine HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology ?_
283	  rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff' _ p (p + 1) (p + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
284	    ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff]
285	  intro x₂ hx₂
286	  have hf : x₂ ≫ R.cocomplex.d (p + 1) (p + 2) = 0 := by
287	    rw [← preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex (p + 1) (p + 2) x₂]
288	    exact hx₂
289	  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := (R.extMk_eq_zero_iff x₂ (p + 2) rfl hf p rfl).mp (hExt _)
290	  refine ⟨g, ?_⟩
291	  rw [← hg]
292	  exact preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex p (p + 1) g
293	
294	/-- **`[HasInjectiveResolutions C]` provides enough injectives.** The degree-`0` term of the chosen
295	injective resolution, with its quasi-iso unit, is an injective presentation. Project-local connector
296	(Mathlib derives `HasInjectiveResolutions` from `EnoughInjectives` + `Abelian`, not conversely);
297	needed to feed `[EnoughInjectives U.Modules]` to the spectrum `Ext`-transport from
298	`[HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]`. -/
299	lemma enoughInjectives_of_hasInjectiveResolutions {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
300	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] : EnoughInjectives C where
301	  presentation X := by
302	    obtain ⟨R⟩ := (HasInjectiveResolutions.out X).out
303	    exact ⟨{ J := R.cocomplex.X 0, injective := R.injective 0, f := R.ι.f 0
304	             mono := CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι R 0 }⟩
305	
306	/-- **An isomorphism in the first `Ext`-argument transfers subsingletons.** If `Ext B Y q` is a
307	subsingleton and `φ : A ≅ B`, then so is `Ext A Y q` (every class is the `φ.hom`-precomposite of its
308	`φ.inv`-precomposite, which vanishes). Project-local: the contravariant `Ext`-functoriality packaged
309	for the Serre-vanishing transport along the `jShriekOU` transport iso. -/
310	lemma subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C]
311	    {A B : C} (φ : A ≅ B) (Y : C) (q : ℕ)
312	    [Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext B Y q)] :
313	    Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q) := by
314	  refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
315	  suffices h : ∀ z : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q, z = 0 by rw [h a, h b]
316	  intro z
317	  have hw : (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q) = 0 := Subsingleton.elim _ _
318	  have hz : z = (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.hom).comp
319	      ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q)) (zero_add q) := by
320	    rw [Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀_assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
321	  rw [hz, hw, Abelian.Ext.comp_zero]
322	
323	/-- **Serre `Ext`-vanishing over an affine scheme via the spectrum transport.** Let `U` be a scheme,
324	`φ : U ≅ Spec R` a scheme isomorphism, `V ⊆ U` an open and `H` an `𝒪_U`-module.  If the pushforward
325	`Φ(j_!𝒪_V)` of the corepresenting object is isomorphic to `j_!𝒪_{V'}` for an *affine* open
326	`V' ⊆ Spec R`, and `Φ H` is quasi-coherent, then every class of `Ext^q_U(j_!𝒪_V, H)` vanishes for
327	`q ≥ 1`.  Assembled from `pushforwardExtAddEquiv` (the spectrum transport), the contravariant
328	`Ext`-transfer `subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst`, and the now-unconditional
329	`affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` on `Spec R`.  Project-local: the Bridge (2) Serre-vanishing
330	leaf of the open-immersion acyclicity, parameterized over the geometric transport data so that the
331	residual `jShriekOU`/quasi-coherence transport sub-lemmas are isolated. -/
332	lemma ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (U : Scheme.{u})
333	    [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules] (φ : U ≅ Spec R) (V : U.Opens) (H : U.Modules)
334	    (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (V' : (Spec R).Opens) (hV' : IsAffineOpen V')
335	    (hjt : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V')
336	    (hqc : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent)
337	    (e : Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) H q) : e = 0 := by
338	  haveI : EnoughInjectives U.Modules := enoughInjectives_of_hasInjectiveResolutions
339	  set Φ := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ with hΦ
340	  haveI : EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules := EnoughInjectives.of_equivalence Φ.inverse
341	  haveI : (Φ.functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := hqc
342	  haveI hsub0 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V') (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
343	    ⟨fun a b => (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq a).trans
344	      (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq b).symm⟩
345	  haveI hsub1 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (Φ.functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
346	    subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst hjt (Φ.functor.obj H) q
347	  have htr := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv φ (jShriekOU V) H q
348	  exact htr.injective (Subsingleton.elim (htr e) (htr 0))

350	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `jShriekOU` transport along a scheme iso (Need #1)
351	
352	The corepresenting object `jShriekOU V` of `Γ(V,-)` is carried along a scheme isomorphism
353	`φ : X ≅ Y` by the induced pushforward equivalence: `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.
354	The argument is pure corepresentability transport — `jShriekOU V` is never unfolded.  Both sides
355	corepresent the *same* `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`: the right side directly (the
356	additive corepresentability `jShriekOU_homEquiv`), the left side via the pushforward adjunction
357	(`Φ.functor ⊣ Φ.inverse`) composed with the definitional identity "sections of a pushforward =
358	sections over the preimage open".  `CorepresentableBy.uniqueUpToIso` then produces the iso. -/
359	
360	/-- **`jShriekOU V` corepresents the `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(V,-)`.**  Repackages the
361	additive corepresentability `jShriekOU_homEquiv` as a `CorepresentableBy` of `sectionsFunctor V`
362	post-composed with the forgetful functor to types. Project-local. -/
363	private noncomputable def sectionsCorep {X : Scheme.{u}} (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
364	    (sectionsFunctor V ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat).CorepresentableBy (jShriekOU V) where
365	  homEquiv {B} := (jShriekOU_homEquiv V B).toEquiv
366	  homEquiv_comp g f := jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V f g
367	
368	/-- **The pushforward of `jShriekOU V` corepresents `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`.**  Via the pushforward
369	adjunction `Φ.functor ⊣ Φ.inverse` and the definitional identity "sections of `φ.inv`-pushforward
370	over `V` = sections over `φ.inv⁻¹V`", the object `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V)` corepresents the same
371	`Type`-valued sections functor as `jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.  Project-local. -/
372	private noncomputable def sectionsCorepPushforward {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
373	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
374	    (sectionsFunctor (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V) ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat).CorepresentableBy
375	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) where
376	  homEquiv {B} :=
377	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).toAdjunction.homEquiv (jShriekOU V) B).trans
378	      (jShriekOU_homEquiv V ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).inverse.obj B)).toEquiv
379	  homEquiv_comp {B B'} g f := by
380	    simp only [Equiv.trans_apply]
381	    rw [(Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).toAdjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
382	    erw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V
383	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).toAdjunction.homEquiv (jShriekOU V) B f)
384	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).inverse.map g)]
385	
386	/-- **A scheme isomorphism carries `jShriekOU` along** (blueprint `lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso`).
387	For `φ : X ≅ Y` and an open `V ⊆ X`, the pushforward equivalence sends the corepresenting object of
388	`Γ(V,-)` to that of `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`:
389	`Φ.functor (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.  Project-local: the geometric naturality of `j_!`
390	under a scheme iso (the first-`Ext`-argument transport of Need #1). -/
391	noncomputable def jShriekOU_transport_along_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
392	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
393	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅
394	      jShriekOU (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V) :=
395	  (sectionsCorepPushforward φ V).uniqueUpToIso (sectionsCorep (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V))
396	
397	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherence transport along a scheme iso (`hqc`) -/
398	
399	/-- **A scheme isomorphism transports an open cover.** If a family `Z` of opens covers `X`
400	(in the sense of the opens Grothendieck topology, i.e. `CoversTop`), then the preimage family
401	`φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ (Z i)` covers `Y`.  Project-local: the cover-transport half of
402	`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` (Need #1 `hqc`). -/
403	private lemma coversTop_preimage_of_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) {I : Type*}
404	    (Z : I → X.Opens) (hZ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).CoversTop Z) :
405	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Z i) := by
406	  intro W x hx
407	  obtain ⟨U', f, hf, hU'⟩ := hZ ⊤ (φ.inv.base x) (by trivial)
408	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
409	  have hxZ : φ.inv.base x ∈ Z i := (leOfHom g) hU'
410	  refine ⟨W ⊓ (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Z i), homOfLE inf_le_left,
411	    ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩, ⟨hx, ?_⟩⟩
412	  exact hxZ

414	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
415	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
416	-- The heartbeat bumps are required: `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` triggers
417	-- synthesis of the doubly-sliced opens `HasSheafify`/`WEqualsLocallyBijective` instances
418	-- `((J.over X).over Y)…`, whose default-heartbeat search times out (it succeeds when bumped).
419	/-- **Reduction of quasi-coherence of an iso-pushforward to the per-slice case** (Need #1 `hqc`,
420	`of_coversTop` wiring). Given a quasi-coherence datum `q` for `H` and quasi-coherence of the
421	restricted pushforward `(φ_* H).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ (q.X i))` over each preimage cover member, the
422	pushforward `φ_* H` is quasi-coherent.  Project-local: wires Mathlib's
423	`IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` to the cover-transport `coversTop_preimage_of_iso`, isolating the
424	residual per-slice obligation. -/
425	lemma pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (H : X.Modules)
426	    (q : SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.{u, u, u, u} H)
427	    (hslice : ∀ i, (((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).over
428	        (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent) :
429	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := by
430	  haveI : ∀ i, (((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).over
431	      (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent := hslice
432	  exact SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop
433	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H) (fun i => φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)
434	    (coversTop_preimage_of_iso φ q.X q.coversTop)

436	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice structure-sheaf ring map `ψ_r` (Need #1 `hqc`)
437	
438	The genuine wall of `hqc`: a single cross-ring slice structure-sheaf ring map `ψ_r` from which the
439	colimit-preserving `SheafOfModules.pullback ψ_r` is built.  For a scheme iso `φ : X ≅ Y` and an open
440	`Uᵢ ⊆ X` with image `Vᵢ = φ.inv⁻¹ᵁ Uᵢ ⊆ Y`, the underlying opens-equivalence functor is
441	`F₀ = Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens X ⥤ Opens Y` (sending `Uᵢ` to `Vᵢ` by `rfl`).  Its slice version
442	`Over.post F₀ : Over Uᵢ ⥤ Over Vᵢ` is continuous and final (both because `F₀` is an equivalence,
443	`φ` being an iso).  The map `ψ_r` is the slice of the structure-sheaf comparison
444	`φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom : 𝒪_X ⟶ (F₀.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _).obj 𝒪_Y`, obtained simply by
445	applying the over-pullback functor `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).overPullback RingCat Uᵢ`; the
446	codomain matches the desired sliced pushforward by the Beck–Chevalley identity
447	`Over.post F₀ ⋙ Over.forget Vᵢ = Over.forget Uᵢ ⋙ F₀` (`rfl`). -/
448	
449	section SlicePsi
450	
451	open TopologicalSpace
452	
453	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) (Ui : X.Opens)
454	
455	/-- `Opens.map φ.inv.base` is an equivalence (its base is the homeomorphism underlying `φ`).
456	Project-local instance feeding the continuity and finality of the slice functor `Over.post`. -/
457	instance opensMapInvBase_isEquivalence : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence :=
458	  inferInstanceAs (Opens.mapMapIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso φ).symm).functor.IsEquivalence
459	
460	/-- The slice functor `Over.post (Opens.map φ.inv.base) : Over Uᵢ ⥤ Over Vᵢ` is continuous for the
461	over-Grothendieck-topologies.  Project-local: from `coverPreserving_opens_map` lifted to the slices
462	via `CoverPreserving.overPost`, plus `compatiblePreservingOfFlat` (the slice functor is flat, being
463	an equivalence). -/
464	instance overPost_slice_isContinuous :
465	    (Over.post (X := Ui) (Opens.map φ.inv.base)).IsContinuous
466	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)
467	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) :=
468	  Functor.isContinuous_of_coverPreserving
469	    (compatiblePreservingOfFlat _ _)
470	    (CoverPreserving.overPost Ui (coverPreserving_opens_map φ.inv.base))
471	
472	set_option linter.style.longLine false in
473	/-- **The slice structure-sheaf ring map `ψ_r`** (blueprint `lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom`).  The
474	over-pullback (to the slice `Over Uᵢ`) of the structure-sheaf comparison `φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom`.
475	Its codomain is the sliced pushforward of `𝒪_Y` along `Over.post (Opens.map φ.inv.base)`, identified
476	with the over-pullback of the un-sliced pushforward by the Beck–Chevalley `rfl`-equality
477	`Over.post F ⋙ Over.forget = Over.forget ⋙ F`.  Project-local: the single cross-ring datum from
478	which `SheafOfModules.pullback ψ_r` (Need #1 `hqc`) is built. -/
479	noncomputable def sliceStructureSheafHom : (X.ringCatSheaf.over Ui) ⟶
480	    ((Over.post (X := Ui) (Opens.map φ.inv.base)).sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
481	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over Ui)
482	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui))).obj
483	        (Y.ringCatSheaf.over (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ Ui)) :=
484	  ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).overPullback RingCat Ui).map (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)
485	
486	/-- The presheaf-of-modules pushforward along `ψ_r.hom` admits a right adjoint (the categories are
487	small).  Project-local instance feeding the sheaf-level right-adjointness. -/
488	instance sliceStructureSheafHom_pre_isRightAdjoint :
489	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui).hom).IsRightAdjoint :=
490	  Functor.isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
491	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui).hom)
492	
493	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
494	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
495	-- The heartbeat bumps are required: the derivation triggers synthesis of the sliced-site
496	-- `HasWeakSheafify`/`WEqualsLocallyBijective` instances for `(gt Y).over Vᵢ`, whose
497	-- default-heartbeat search times out (it succeeds when bumped).
498	/-- **`SheafOfModules.pushforward ψ_r` admits a right adjoint** — the obligation that makes the
499	left-adjoint pullback `SheafOfModules.pullback ψ_r` available (blueprint
500	`lem:slice_structureSheaf_hom`, the `IsRightAdjoint` clause).  Project-local instance. -/
501	instance sliceStructureSheafHom_isRightAdjoint :
502	    (SheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom φ Ui)).IsRightAdjoint :=
503	  inferInstance
504	
505	end SlicePsi
